\brag pages
About Submit

About

What is a /brag page? Who came up with the idea? How can I submit my /brag page? How can I remove my /brag page? How does this website work? So... I have a suggestion! What is a /brag page? Who came up with the idea? How can I submit my /brag page? How can I remove my /brag page? How does this website work? So... I have a suggestion!

What is a /brag page?

A /brag page is a personal page where you tell people about your professional wins(*): what have you shipped, what are you definetely the best at, what excites the most about your work? It is an evolving and opiniated record of your career.

This is a collection of all /brag pages submitted by users.

Isn't that what that blue app does?

Not even close. That app is a platform that owns your data, curates your presentation and buries your wins in an algorithm. A /brag page is yours - your domain, your format, your voice. Do it as you please. Share it with us, please.

Who came up with \brag pages?

Aaron Francis, the 2x dad of twins and father of Solo (not a Solo dad though, kudos to Jenifer) and Ian Landsman, The Business Dad, initiated the talks about brag pages on episode 132 of The Mostly Technical Podcast.

Listen to Mostly Technical Podcast

Who built this website?

This website was built by Dandara, a mostly technical designer.

Check out her portfolio Read more about this website development

How to submit a /brag page?

Open a pull request on the GitHub repo and add your entry to brags.json. The submit page has all the details.

How to remove my page from the list?

To remove your page from the list, simply open an issue on GitHub or better yet open a PR removing your entry from the data file.

How does this website work?

I could tell you, but better yet, I could show you! It is open source, have a blast digging the code on GitHub.

So... I have a suggestion!

That's awesome! You can either open an issue - or better yet, a PR - on GitHub or @ me on Twitter (@amarelodandara), the everything app.
